topic: Chen et al.'s (arXiv:2510.22711, 2025) higher-cumulant rank-deficiency direction test under m latent confounders: minimal truncation order and exceptional non-identification locus.

open_problems.open_problem: Determine the information-theoretic minimal joint-cumulant truncation order for generic causal-direction identification between two observed variables under m latent confounders, including lower-order opposite-arrow twins.

source_refs.claim: Establishes direction identification from rank deficiency of a specifically constructed higher-order joint-cumulant matrix with arbitrary latent confounding, but does not establish a sharp minimal truncation order or describe the one-order-lower reverse-arrow fiber.
source_refs.author: Wei Chen, Linjun Peng, Zhiyi Huang, Haoyue Dai, Zhifeng Hao, Ruichu Cai, Kun Zhang

source_refs.excerpt: construct two generic laws matching all cumulants up to order m while separating lambda_uv.
m_star is not an information-theoretic lower frontier for all cumulant functionals.

open_problems.why_unsolved: Chen et al. supply a sufficient rank-deficiency construction, whereas the predecessor shows why a witness-specific order cannot be treated as a global lower bound: a different observable functional can identify the same target at lower order. A sharp result must therefore quantify over every reverse-arrow parameterization and every observable cumulant functional, not merely show failure of the anchor's chosen rank witness.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An information-order theorem specifying the least K for generic arrow recovery, with an explicit family of opposite-arrow laws agreeing through K-1 and a generic recovery map at K.

writing_exemplar.why: It supplies the exact two-variable, arbitrary-latent-confounder motivation arc and the rank-deficiency formulation to sharpen.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: latent confounding defeats ordinary LiNGAM orientation -> higher-order joint cumulant ranks reveal directional asymmetry -> what is the least cumulant order at which that asymmetry is generically decisive?

method_exemplars.technique: Use generic rank conditions on higher-order cumulant matrices and algebraically compare compatible latent-confounded structural representations.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The required lower-order twin and recovery arguments are comparisons of finite cumulant-image fibers under competing structural representations.

method_exemplars.technique: Estimate a fixed finite vector of multivariate cumulants and propagate its joint asymptotic covariance through smooth algebraic functionals.
method_exemplars.why_closest: A minimal-order identifying functional admits a sample-cumulant plug-in estimator once its nonzero discriminant regime is stated.

open_problems.open_problem: Obtain a uniformly valid finite-sample causal-direction test for the rank-deficiency criterion on a stated determinant or singular-value margin away from the cumulant-image intersection locus.

source_refs.claim: Shows that latent-confounded LiNGAM orientation can be consistent in the limit while lacking finite-sample error bounds without additional restrictions, so any valid test must state a margin/restriction class.
source_refs.author: Konstantin Genin

source_refs.claim: Claims asymptotic correctness for a noniterative higher-order-cumulant direction method under arbitrary latent confounders, leaving a finite-sample margin-based error theorem outside the stated identification result.
source_refs.author: Wei Chen, Linjun Peng, Zhiyi Huang, Haoyue Dai, Zhifeng Hao, Ruichu Cai, Kun Zhang

source_refs.excerpt: Weak-denominator uniform coverage is explicitly outside scope.

open_problems.why_unsolved: Genin's impossibility rules out unqualified finite-sample decidability under latent confounding, while the available higher-cumulant methods establish asymptotic rather than margin-uniform guarantees. Near an intersection of the two cumulant-image varieties the rank discriminant is weak, so a pointwise delta method cannot control erroneous orientation uniformly; the missing ingredient is a quantitative separation condition and its concentration analysis.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A nonasymptotic or uniform local-asymptotic theorem bounding direction-selection error and giving a confidence-set/test-inversion procedure over laws whose cumulant-image separation has singular value at least a stated margin.

writing_exemplar.why: Its framing cleanly distinguishes population identifiability from finite-sample statistical decidability and makes the needed restriction explicit.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: LiNGAM can orient arrows from observational laws -> latent confounding destroys unrestricted finite-sample decidability -> a quantitative cumulant-separation margin may recover valid testing on a restricted class

method_exemplars.technique: Formulate orientation selection as a uniform testing problem and prove exactly where finite-sample control fails without a restricted model class.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The proposed margin is precisely the additional restriction required to avoid the unrestricted impossibility.

method_exemplars.technique: Use the constructed joint higher-order cumulant matrix and its rank-deficiency asymmetry as the test statistic's population target.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Its directional rank functional is the object whose margin and sampling fluctuation must be controlled.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize exactly when a latent-confounded linear SEM admits a finite higher-order observable cumulant transfer that separates structural alternatives despite bow-induced connected-set contamination.

source_refs.excerpt: Promote the transfer-search handle into an explicit algorithm with input format, admissibility test
find a graph class + explicit m>2 augmentation that beats the monotone-bow block

open_problems.why_unsolved: The prior run exposed a concrete obstruction: augmenting cumulant arguments enough to make a desired row nonzero can retain a generically nonzero bow-contamination term, so its natural monotone construction cannot certify cancellation. Existing higher-cumulant proxy and underspecified-IV formulas are hand-tailored subclasses, not a criterion deciding whether a finite separating transfer exists for a given confounded graph.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient graphical or algebraic admissibility theorem with a terminating construction, or an impossibility certificate, for a finite cumulant tensor decomposition yielding an observable direction-separating functional.

writing_exemplar.why: It motivates higher-order cumulant equations through concrete latent-confounded proxy and IV identification cases.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: latent confounding blocks ordinary LiNGAM effect recovery -> higher-order cumulants solve structured proxy/IV cases -> which general confounded graphs admit a finite observable cumulant transfer?

method_exemplars.technique: Construct a cross-moment ratio whose proof explicitly cancels latent nuisance contributions.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The desired criterion generalizes the cancellation mechanism of the single-proxy prototype.

method_exemplars.technique: Couple finite higher-order cumulant equations with sample-cumulant plug-in estimation.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Any constructive transfer certificate produces the same finite-dimensional cumulant-equation estimation problem.

open_problems.open_problem: Give necessary and sufficient finite-cumulant conditions for bivariate arrow identification when the latent confounders themselves have an acyclic dependency structure, rather than being mutually independent.

source_refs.claim: Extends higher-order-cumulant causal discovery to LvLiNGAMs with causal relations among latent and observed variables, but its algorithmic assumptions do not furnish a sharp bivariate necessary-and-sufficient finite-cumulant arrow criterion.
source_refs.author: Ming Cai, Penggang Gao, Hisayuki Hara

source_refs.claim: Uses higher-order joint cumulant rank deficiency to orient a two-variable relation under arbitrary latent confounders in a linear non-Gaussian setting.
source_refs.author: Wei Chen, Linjun Peng, Zhiyi Huang, Haoyue Dai, Zhifeng Hao, Ruichu Cai, Kun Zhang

open_problems.why_unsolved: Dependence among latent variables creates mixed cumulants that no longer factor as independent-component contributions, so the binary-form/rank arguments used for independent confounders do not directly apply. The recent dependent-latent work demonstrates algorithmic progress but does not isolate the exact two-observed-variable compatibility geometry needed for a finite-order necessary-and-sufficient orientation result.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient finite-cumulant identification theorem, with an explicit functional or rank criterion and a matching reverse-arrow construction, for a bivariate LvLiNGAM whose latent subgraph belongs to a stated acyclic dependency class.

writing_exemplar.why: It gives the closest motivation arc for relaxing independent-latent LiNGAM while retaining higher-order cumulants.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: independent-latent assumptions exclude causally related hidden causes -> higher-order cumulants can handle broader dependent-latent DAGs -> identify exactly when the bivariate observed arrow remains recoverable

method_exemplars.technique: Derive structural restrictions from observed higher-order cumulants while accounting for latent causal relations.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The target retains the same dependent-latent cumulant algebra but specializes it to an exact two-variable characterization.

method_exemplars.technique: Estimate higher-order cumulant rank matrices and prove asymptotic correctness of a recursive latent-confounded discovery procedure.
method_exemplars.why_closest: A finite bivariate criterion needs a concrete sample-cumulant rank-estimation rung after the population characterization.

literature_map:
1. Chen, Peng, Huang et al. (2025, arXiv:2510.22711) is the direct anchor: higher-order joint-cumulant rank deficiency orients two variables under arbitrary latent confounders.
2. The immediate unresolved calibration is sharpness: its constructed sufficient order does not itself prove minimality or enumerate the lower-order reverse-arrow fiber.
3. Chen, Huang, Cai et al. (2024, AAAI, DOI 10.1609/aaai.v38i18.30017) gives the earlier one-latent cumulant-asymmetry prototype.
4. Cai, Huang, Chen et al. (2023, ICML/OpenReview: O1Hn6YF5IF) develops higher-cumulant discovery under a One-Latent-Component condition.
5. Schkoda, Robeva and Drton (2024, arXiv:2408.04907) uses higher-order cumulant rank matrices for latent-confounded LiNGAM discovery and finite compatible effect sets.
6. Tramontano, Drton and Etesami (2024/2026 revision, arXiv:2405.20856) gives generic parameter-identification machinery under general confounding, but not a sharp cumulant-order frontier.
7. Tramontano, Kivva, Salehkaleybar, Drton and Kiyavash (2025, arXiv:2506.05202) identifies effects in selected proxy and underspecified-IV lvLiNGAM regimes via higher cumulants.
8. Kivva, Salehkaleybar and Kiyavash (2023, arXiv:2306.06263) is the low-dimensional cross-moment-ratio/cancellation prototype.
9. Genin (2021, NeurIPS) proves that latent-confounded LiNGAM can be asymptotically orientable yet fail unrestricted finite-sample statistical decidability.
10. Thus a testing theorem must add a quantitative cumulant-separation margin rather than claim uniform validity over all confounded laws.
11. Cai, Gao and Hara (2025, arXiv:2510.14780) extends cumulant-based discovery to dependent latent variables, opening a separate exact bivariate-characterization problem.
12. The enduring tension is between algebraic generic identification from a finite cumulant image and nonregular or nearly intersecting image varieties, where rank tests are weak.
13. The closest field-facing route is an explicit simultaneous binary-form/finite-cumulant fiber analysis, not a new causal-discovery completeness algorithm.
14. The four retained gaps span sharp order, margin-based inference, finite transfer admissibility, and dependent-latent extension; only the first directly matches the supplied minimal-order kernel.
prior_proposal_map:
1. The current qid directory contains state and pipeline initialization only; it has no prior proposal or review to mine.
2. The closest predecessor is downgraded `eid_lnsem_cumulant_id_v1`, a linear non-Gaussian SEM higher-cumulant identification run.
3. Its reviewer `reviews/angle0_v2.json` prescribed a lower-bound construction matching all finite cumulants while separating the target.
4. The same review requested an explicit transfer-search algorithm with input format and admissibility test.
5. Its README records the concrete monotone-bow-contamination obstruction and requests either an m>2 construction or a necessary-and-sufficient criterion.
6. Its writeup establishes that its witness-specific m_star is not an information-theoretic lower frontier because another observable formula may identify at lower order.
7. The reviewer explicitly excluded weak-denominator uniform coverage, furnishing the high-signal inference-boundary gap.
8. No qid-specific rejected-angle TeX was present; unrelated archived EID material was excluded.
